front: What is a rooted tree in graph theory?
back: A rooted tree is a tree data structure where one specific node is designated as the root, and every edge is directed away from the root, giving the tree a hierarchical structure.

front: Define the root node of a tree.
back: The root is the unique topmost node of a tree that has no parent. All other nodes are descendants of the root node.

front: What is a leaf node (terminal node)?
back: A leaf node is a node with no children (degree 1 in directed tree from parent perspective). It is also called a terminal node or external node.

front: Define the height of a tree.
back: The height of a tree is the length of the longest path from the root to a leaf node. It can also be defined as the maximum level number (root at level 0).

front: What is the depth of a node?
back: The depth of a node is the length of the path from the root to that node. The root has depth 0.
A binary tree is a rooted tree in which each node has at most two children, typically referred to as the left child and the right child.
A complete binary tree is a tree in which all levels except possibly the last are completely filled, and all nodes are as far left as possible.

front: What is a full (proper) binary tree?
back: A full binary tree is a binary tree in which every node has either 0 or 2 children. No node has exactly one child.
A perfect binary tree is a binary tree in which all internal nodes have exactly two children and all leaf nodes are at the same level.

front: What is a binary search tree (BST)?
back: A binary search tree is a binary tree where each node's left subtree contains only values less than the node, and the right subtree contains only values greater than the node.
Tree traversal is the process of visiting all nodes in a tree systematically. Common traversals include preorder, inorder, postorder, and level order.
Inorder traversal visits nodes in order: left subtree, root, right subtree. For BST, it gives nodes in sorted order.
Preorder traversal visits nodes in order: root, left subtree, right subtree. Useful for creating tree copies.
Postorder traversal visits nodes in order: left subtree, right subtree, root. Useful for deleting trees or evaluating expressions.

front: What is the maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h?
back: The maximum number of nodes is 2^(h+1) - 1, where height h has (h+1) levels (levels 0 to h). For a tree with n levels, max nodes = 2^n - 1.
